name: Judith Olney's Chocolate Buttermilk Cake
recipeInstructions:
I. THE CAKE:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Grease 3 round, 9-inch cake pans. Line the bottoms with greased wax paper.
2. Sift together flour, cocoa, baking soda, baking powder, and cinnamon. Mix in buttermilk and coffee (dissolve coffee crystals in an equal amount of hot water first).
3. Cream butter with sugar until light and fluffy. Add eggs one at a time until thick. Beat on low speed, then mix in the buttermilk mixture. Beat until well blended. Bake about 35 minutes, or until a straw goes through the center easily.
4. Remove from oven to racks to cool. After they are cooled, put them in the refrigerator. After 1 hour in the refrigerator, split each cake in half (see note at bottom). Wrap each layer in wax paper or plastic wrap and freeze immediately.
II. SOUR CREAM FILLING:
1. Beat heavy cream with sugar until stiff.
2. Gently fold in sour cream. Set aside in the refrigerator (covered).
III. CHOCOLATE SOUR CREAM FROSTING:
1. Melt remaining butter. Add 1/2 cup powdered sugar and remaining cocoa. Stir with a wire whisk over low heat until smooth; cool.
2. Beat remaining heavy cream; add remaining powdered sugar until soft peaks form. Add cooled chocolate mixture; beat until stiff. Fold in remaining sour cream.
IV. CONSTRUCTION:
1. Build layers from the bottom up as follows: Cake, 1/3 the filling, cake, 2/3 frosting.
2. Repeat above twice for a total of six layers.
3. Put remaining frosting on sides.
4. Chill two hours before serving.
Note: The cakes must be well-chilled before assembly or they will disintegrate under their own weight during assembly. If the cake tops rose during baking, the layers will not stack well. You will need to slice off the excess after chilling but before splitting into layers.
